title: Jordan Clarkson One Win Away From Filipino NBA History
summary: The New York Knicks lead the San Antonio Spurs 3-1 in the 2026 NBA Finals after a record-breaking 29-point comeback. Jordan Clarkson is on the verge of becoming the first player of Filipino ancestry to win an NBA championship.
content:
The New York Knicks are one win away from an NBA championship, and the entire Philippines is watching.

**Jordan Clarkson**, the Filipino-American guard who has long been a source of national pride, is on the cusp of making history. If the Knicks close out the San Antonio Spurs in Game 5 at Madison Square Garden on June 14, Clarkson becomes the **first player of Filipino ancestry to ever win an NBA title**. And honestly, the way this series has gone, it feels like destiny.

The Knicks took a commanding 3-1 series lead after pulling off the **biggest comeback in NBA Finals history** — erasing a 29-point deficit in Game 4. Let that sink in. Twenty-nine points down, and they clawed their way back in front of a raucous MSG crowd. It was the kind of game that reminds you why basketball is the greatest sport on the planet.

Clarkson himself had a moment to remember in that Game 4 thriller. He dropped **10 points on 57.1% shooting**, providing a crucial spark off the bench when the Knicks desperately needed one. For the series, he's averaging 3.6 points and 1.8 rebounds — modest numbers, sure, but his impact goes way beyond the box score. He's the guy who hit that **unreal Hail Mary three in Game 3** that had everyone losing their minds.

And then there was OG Anunoby's legendary tip-in with 4.5 seconds left in Game 4 — the play that sealed the comeback and sent New York into absolute delirium. Anunoby, ever the humble one, downplayed the heroics afterward. But that shot will be replayed for decades.

**The Philippines has fully embraced Clarkson as one of their own.** He suited up for Gilas Pilipinas at the 2018 Asian Games, where he averaged a jaw-dropping 26 points per game. That performance cemented his status as a cultural icon — not just a role player on a contender, but a symbol of what Filipino athletes can achieve on the world's biggest stage.

The road to Game 5 hasn't been without drama off the court either. The MSG watch party was initially cancelled due to a security clash between Knicks owner James Dolan and NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani, but it was reinstated — meaning fans will pack the Garden's surroundings to witness what could be a historic night.

On the other side, the Spurs aren't going quietly. **Victor Wembanyama**, fresh off being named one of TIME100's Most Influential People in Sports for 2026, is still the most dominant force in basketball. San Antonio will fight to send this series back to Texas. But the Knicks have all the momentum, all the belief, and a 29-point comeback to prove that nothing is impossible.

For Filipino basketball fans around the world, this is more than just a Finals series. **This is a moment generations in the making.** Clarkson carrying that Filipino flag to an NBA championship would be a victory not just for the Knicks, but for every kid in Manila, Cebu, and Davao who ever dreamed of seeing someone who looks like them holding that Larry O'Brien Trophy.

Game 5. Madison Square Garden. June 14. History awaits.
seo_description: Jordan Clarkson and the Knicks are one win away from the 2026 NBA title. Victory would make Clarkson the first Filipino-American NBA champion.
image_prompt: A dramatic wide shot of Madison Square Garden at night during an NBA Finals game, with the court glowing under bright arena lights, a Filipino-American player in a New York Knicks jersey holding a basketball aloft, confetti falling, and a massive crowd on their feet cheering wildly, cinematic sports photography style, golden and blue lighting
